proposals.title: Star Seekers 2 — Mobile Game DAO Funding Proposal
proposals.author: PG Metaverse OG
proposals.proposal_text:
# 1. Star Seekers 2 — Mobile Game DAO Funding Proposal

* Author: PG Metaverse OG
* Type: Ecosystem fund
* Date: March 2026

___
 
# 2. SUMMARY

This proposal requests 13,000,000 ATLAS from the Star Atlas DAO Ecosystem Fund to complete and launch Star Seekers 2, a cross-platform mobile space-combat game inspired by the Star Atlas universe, on both iOS App Store and Google Play Store.

The game builds on the existing Star Seekers ecosystem (Star Seekers 1 on iOS, Coloring Book on Amazon, active Discord community) and is already in an advanced stage of development. This proposal funds the final 2 months needed to complete content, polish, and launch on both platforms.


---
 
# 3. MOTIVATION

Star Seekers 2 acts as a direct, always-on marketing channel for the Star Atlas universe on the two largest mobile marketplaces in the world. Every new player is a first introduction to Star Atlas — no login friction, no blockchain complexity.

Without DAO backing, this level of development and outreach is not sustainable for an independent creator. With funding, Star Seekers becomes a self-sustaining funnel: DAO funds → development → marketing → new players → ecosystem growth.


---

# 4. SPECIFICATION

## 4.1 Deliverables

* Star Seekers 2 — cross-platform game (Flutter + Flame), iOS and Android
* 100 progressive missions with integrated Star Atlas lore
* Custom ships, five collectible artifacts, and a full upgrade/inventory system
* Firebase leaderboards with cross-platform rankings
* Free-to-play with optional in-app purchases (ships, consumables, continues)
* TikTok account management promoting the game and Star Atlas universe
* Star Atlas DAO publicly credited across both apps and store listings

## 4.2 Success Metrics 

* All 100 missions completed, tested, and balanced
* iOS and Android versions publicly live on their respective stores
* Leaderboards and in-app purchases functional on both platforms
* Smooth 60fps performance across target devices
* No critical bugs at launch; positive store ratings

## 4.3 Technical Stack

* Flutter + Flame (cross-platform game engine)
* Firebase Realtime Database (leaderboards, anonymous auth)
* Xcode / Android Studio for platform builds
* AI tools: Claude Code, wan 2.6 image generation


___

# 5. STAKEHOLDER IMPACT

## 5.1 Positive Impacts

* Star Atlas DAO — Converts treasury funding into a long-term user acquisition channel on the App Store (1.56B iPhone users) and Google Play (2.5B+ Android users)
* Broader Ecosystem — Places Star Atlas IP in front of mainstream mobile audiences without blockchain friction, increasing brand visibility and new player conversion
* Community — Provides substantial, lore-rich gameplay (100 missions) that deepens engagement and increases likelihood of conversion to the broader Star Atlas ecosystem

## 5.2 Challenges

* Aggressive 2-month timeline — Manageable given the advanced development state; developer committed to full-time focused work with clear weekly targets
* Staggered platform launch — Android's mandatory testing period is a platform constraint, not a development issue; both builds complete simultaneously
* Dual platform coordination — Flutter's shared codebase minimizes platform-specific overhead

---

# 6. IMPLEMENTATION Details

The game is approximately 60–70% complete. Core systems — combat, leaderboards, IAP framework, authentication, and basic progression — are fully operational. This proposal funds the remaining work across two months.

## 6.1 Month 1 — Content Completion & Polish

 
* Complete all 100 missions with unique layouts, objectives, and balanced difficulty
* Finalize inventory management, enemy AI, and progression/unlock systems
* Finish all five collectible artifacts, remaining enemy types, and background variations

## 6.2 Month 2 — Testing, Optimization & Launch

* Performance and memory optimization across iOS and Android devices
* Internal testing of all 100 missions; bug fixes and stability improvements
* iOS: App Store submission (Week 7), Apple review (1–3 days), public launch
* Android: 14-day mandatory community testing (Week 7–8), production review (3–5 days), public launch (Week 9)

## 6.3 Risks & Mitigations

* 100-mission completion — Month 1 fully dedicated to content, built in batches with progressive testing
* Performance at scale — Weeks 5–6 dedicated to optimization; continuous profiling throughout
* App Store review delays — Buffer time included; pre-submission compliance audits minimize resubmissions
* Android extended timeline — Google Play mandates 14 days of closed testing before production review; Android launches ~17–19 days after iOS. Community testing begins concurrent with iOS launch to minimize overall delay
* Inventory & game logic complexity — Systems built and tested incrementally in Weeks 2–3


___

# 7. Timeline

Development begins within 1 week of PIP approval.

| Phase | Timing | Key Deliverables|
| --- | --- | ---|
| Content Completion | Weeks 1–4 | 100 missions, inventory system, game logic, lore, animations |
| Optimization & Polish | Weeks 5–6 | Performance optimization, final UI/UX, internal beta testing |
| iOS Launch | Weeks 7–8 | App Store submission, Apple review, public iOS launch |
| Android Testing | Weeks 7–8 | 14-day community testing (concurrent with iOS launch) |
| Android Launch | Week 9 | Production review, public Android launch |


---
# 8. Milestones & Payment

This PIP uses a three-milestone structure, with the upfront payment capped at the delivery of the current working product.

| Milestone | Timing | ATLAS | Deliverable |
| --- | --- | ---| ---|
| Milestone 1 | Pre-approval | 0 | Delivery of current working product (build demonstrating core systems) |
| Milestone 2 | PIP Acceptance | 6,500,000 | 50% released upon DAO approval of this proposal |
| Milestone 3 | Week 9 | 6,500,000 | Both platforms publicly launched — iOS + Android live |

 Total DAO Treasury Request: 13,000,000 ATLAS
 
## 8.1 Cost Breakdown

| Category | ATLAS | USD (est.) | % |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Developer Compensation | 10,967,021 | ~$2,062 | 84.4% |
| Claude Code (2 months) | 1,095,745 | ~$206 | 8.4% |
| AI Image Generation (2 months) | 212,766 | ~$40 | 1.6% |
| Canva Pro (2 months) | 159,574 | ~$30 | 1.2% |
| Apple Developer Program | 531,915 | $100 | 4.1% |
| Google Play Account | 132,979 | ~$25 | 1.0% |
| Firebase (absorbed in budget) | - | ~$10–20 | - |
| TOTAL | 13,000,000 | ~$2,444 | 100% |

ATLAS/USD calculated at $0.000188 per ATLAS.


## 8.2 Post-Launch Sustainability

Ongoing costs will be covered by in-app purchase revenue (ships, consumables, continues) under a free-to-play model. Major expansions may be subject to future DAO proposals.

___

# 9 Additional Details

## 9.1 Conflicts of Interest

None. PG Metaverse OG has no financial or organizational ties to the Star Atlas Foundation, ATMTA, or any ecosystem infrastructure providers. All funding flows through the DAO Treasury directly to the developer.

## 9.2 Ownership & Rights

All code, assets, and IP remain the property of PG Metaverse OG. The Star Atlas DAO will be credited as funding sponsor across both apps, store listings, and community channels. This arrangement ensures the creator can support, update, and expand the game indefinitely.

All existing Star Atlas branding and IP remains the property of ATMTA, Inc.

## 9.3 Earlier Versions

This is a revised version of earlier PIPs. Scope has been narrowed to mobile-only (removing the web hub), the timeline is based on actual development status (~60–70% complete), and the payment structure has been updated to three milestones per DAO reviewer feedback.

proposals.title: Igniting the Star Atlas Triad Tournament - A Super Phoenix Sports Live Esports Event
proposals.author: Money, Lanzer, Njord
proposals.proposal_text:
# Igniting the Star Atlas Triad Tournament - A Super Phoenix Sports Live Esports Event

* Author: Money, Lanzer, Njord
* Type: Ecosystem fund
* Date: April 2026

___

## 2. SUMMARY
This proposal seeks funding of the Ecosystem Fund limit (currently 13,576,392 ATLAS) for the production and broadcast of the Star Atlas Triad Tournament in June 2026, a first-ever combined competitive event spanning all three UE5 game modes: Racing Mode (Star Racer), Gun Mode (Star Shooter), Siege Mode (Star Fighter). Funding is to compensate the team only, it is not funding a prize pool.

Produced by the Super Phoenix Sports (SPS) team, this mega-tournament will be the most ambitious competitive Star Atlas event ever attempted and will be broadcast live across multiple platforms to a global audience. VODs available here: https://www.youtube.com/@superphoenixsports

Since Sep 2025, SPS has:

* Hosted six tournament events uniting DACs, rival factions, and external Web3 communities around competitive Star Atlas gameplay. 
* Two large-scale “Clash of Titans” tournaments featuring UE5 Gun Mode and Racing Mode ranked in the top 1% of Twitch and top 10% of YouTube livestreamers in Dec 2025 and Apr 2026. 
* Over 1,000 viewers tuned in across four platforms (Twitch, X Broadcast, Kick, YouTube Live). 
* More than 40 Star Atlas community players have competed. 
* SPS has paid out over $5,000 in USDC and $8,000 in ship NFTs in prize pools, almost entirely from its own resources.

By the end of May 2026, SPS will have hosted 10 or more tournament events.

This proposal asks the DAO to back the next chapter. A unified three-mode championship is the kind of marquee live event that elevates Star Atlas from a game with a competitive scene into a legitimate esport. It brings new eyes to the ecosystem, rewards active players, and demonstrates to the broader gaming world that Star Atlas has arrived.
___

## 3. MOTIVATION
The DAO's stated long-term vision is a player-owned, community-driven metaverse. Competitive live events are the most powerful tools available to build that community. This is how we get there.

Star Atlas has a live esports audience. The viewership of SPS tournaments proves it. The community has responded. The audience is real. The infrastructure exists.

Livestream history
* 621 live viewers across 4 events in April 2026
* 839 live viewers across 5 events in March 2026

___

## 4. SPECIFICATION
The Star Atlas Triad Tournament is a single-day championship event featuring three consecutive competitive brackets across Star Atlas's UE5 game modes:

* Star Racer - UE5 Racing Mode bracket
* Star Shooter - UE5 Gun Mode bracket
* Star Fighter - UE5 Siege Mode bracket

Each bracket operates as a standalone competition with its own prize allocation, seeding, and format. Combined scores or a points system across all three brackets will determine an overall Triad Champion.

The event will be broadcast live across Twitch, YouTube, and additional streaming platforms with professional commentary, overlays, and production quality consistent with SPS's prior events. A recorded highlight package will be distributed across Star Atlas and SPS social channels following the event.

Participation will be open to Star Atlas community members, including: DAC members, independent players, and invited Web3 community representatives.

Prize pool distribution will be drawn from outside fundraising.

___

## 5. STAKEHOLDER IMPACT
Star Atlas Players - Positively impacted. Competitive players gain a high-stakes, high-visibility event to participate in and rewarding prize pool.

POLIS Holders and DAO Members - Positively impacted. A successful broadcast event with strong viewership drives awareness of Star Atlas and increases the perceived value of the ecosystem. DAO funding is deployed toward community-visible output with measurable results.

Potential Negative Impact - The funding request represents a draw on Ecosystem Fund during a bear market.

Non-Competing Community Members - Viewers and community members who do not compete still benefit from the content and community energy generated by the event.
ATMTA and the Star Atlas DAO - Positively impacted. Third-party esports production at no cost to ATMTA amplifies the game's visibility at a critical moment in the UE5 launch cycle.

External Web3 and Gaming Communities - Positively impacted. SPS has a track record of bringing outside communities into Star Atlas events, expanding the game's reach beyond its existing player base. Examples include: SolGG, Decrypted.tax, Dream Test Labs

___

## 6. IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS
Upon approval of this PIP, the following steps will be executed in order:

* SPS confirms internal production team assignments and finalizes event format across all three brackets.
* SPS publishes official event announcements and broadcasts schedule across Star Atlas Discord, X, and SPS channels.
* Player registration opens. SPS manages registration, seeding, and bracket construction.
* SPS conducts a pre-event test broadcast to confirm technical setup across all streaming platforms.
* The Star Atlas Triad Tournament is hosted and broadcast live.

The DAO will continue to be featured throughout the tournament

Prize pool is distributed to winners following event completion.

SPS publishes a post-event report to the DAO, including: viewership numbers, participation data, and a full accounting of fund usage.

SPS is the responsible party for all production, broadcast, registration, and prize distribution. The event operates entirely on existing UE5 game modes.

Hosting Solution: Live broadcast across Twitch, X, and additional platforms. Event coordination managed via SPS internal infrastructure and existing community channels.

Potential Challenges: 

* Scheduling. Player availability and scheduling across time zones will be managed through advance registration and clearly published bracket timelines. Low registration will be addressed through active community outreach beginning immediately upon PIP approval using previously established best practices by the SPS Team.
* State of the Game Modes. Different game modes are at various degrees of completion. There are known bugs that make hosting a tournament difficult (examples: invincibility bug, game crashing, 5-minute game start delay bug, and spectator camera bugs). These bugs have caused previous tournaments to be postponed. As of Apr 12, 2026: Gun Mode is stable enough to host weekly tournaments, Racing Mode is hostable but not entirely stable, and Siege Mode has multiple stability issues that may not be fixed before the Triad Tournament is scheduled to be held. SPS Team and the Star Atlas development team are in constant communication about feature requests to improve the state of the game. Should any one game mode prove too unstable to host a quality tournament event (examples: if spectator camera doesn’t work or lobby won’t start the match), SPS Team will need to either delay the tournament or rescope the tournament as a compromise. It will be the responsibility of the SPS Team to determine this. Examples of previous mitigation techniques: creating redundant video tracks to stream, screenshotting match results, players share screens with SPS Referee, restarting matches where fair play or game integrity is broken.

A return on investment (ROI) is offered to give back to the DAO. See Section 8.

___

## 7. TIMELINE
Projected Timeline

* PIP Approval: Late-May 2026 
* Event Announcement and Registration Open: Within 7 days of PIP vote concluding
* Pre-Event Test Broadcast: One week prior to event
* Star Atlas Triad Tournament Live Event: End of June 2026. Per potential challenges noted in paragraph 6(f)(ii). 
* Start within 1 month of a fix to Siege Mode being made, note: Per PIP-23 “Refresh of Ecosystem Fund”, a PIP may not extend for more than 1 year. If a delay is needed, then the event has up to 6 months to complete.
* Prize Distribution: Within 7 days of event completion 
* Post-Event DAO Report: Within 14 days of event completion
* Payment Schedule: Please see below proposed milestone payments

Milestones

* Conclude PIP Vote (25% payment): If PIP concludes with a majority YES vote, then initial payment is requested no later than 5 days after PIP vote concludes.
* Complete Tournament Event (50% payment): Not later than 1 year from date PIP vote concludes.
* Publish Results Report (25% payment): No later than 14 days after the event concludes

___

## 8. COST & RESOURCE ALLOCATION
One-Time Costs: 13,576,392 ATLAS

* Operations and Broadcast - ATLAS will be allocated to personnel of SPS Team and required infrastructure (multi-platform streaming requirements and technical stack). The funded amount compensates approximately 20 staff members.

* Funding is to compensate the team only, it is not funding a prize pool.

Recurring Cost: 0 ATLAS

* N/A

Total Requested: Ecosystem Fund limit, 13,576,392 ATLAS

Source of Funding: Star Atlas DAO Ecosystem Fund, PIP-23

* Alternative Funding: SPS has self-funded over $13K USDC in prior prize pools, personnel, and production costs across six events. This proposal represents the first DAO funding request made by SPS. Sponsorship outreach to external Web3 partners is ongoing and may supplement future events.
* Ongoing Costs: N/A. This proposal covers a single event. Future events will be subject to separate proposals or alternative funding arrangements.

Return on Investment (ROI). 

* Even during the current bear market, esports viewership is strong. SPS has activated the larger web3 community through not only player participation, but also sponsorships, advertisements, and bracket sweepstakes. The SPS Team wants the DAO to share in its success.
* SPS will give 1% of revenue earned from the Triad Tournament back to the DAO.

___

## 9. ADDITIONAL DETAILS
* Super Phoenix Sports is powered by Super Phoenix DAO (SPD), a longstanding diamond hands member of the Star Atlas community and owner of the Super Phoenix (https://superphoenixdao.com/landing). This conflict of interest is mitigated through SPS being a separately registered company and transparent post-event reporting.
* Lanzer is currently the Director of the SPS Team. He also serves on the DAO Council (SAC-3) and is co-author of this PIP. Lanzer will recuse himself from all necessary activities where an impartial decision is needed as council-member (milestone payment release vote, SAC-3 vote for PIP to proceed to Foundation review).
* Morango is currently on the SPS Team. She also serves on the DAO Council (SAC-3). She will recuse herself from all necessary activities where an impartial decision is needed as council-member (milestone payment release vote, SAC-3 vote for PIP to proceed to Foundation review).

Earlier Versions: This proposal has not been previously submitted or rejected. Separate SPS Tournament Events have been previously funded under PIP-22 “Iris Bounty: The Arena” events. Cost to produce tournaments greatly exceeds the Iris Bounty Arena max funding limit of ~$250, and historical revenue has been less than $1K per month, which resulted in this PIP.

Reference: Past SPS broadcast available for community review at https://www.youtube.com/@superphoenixsports

Power to the People.

Funding is to compensate the team only, it is not funding a prize pool.

proposals.title: ATMTA Historic Expense Reimbursement
proposals.author: Michael Wagner, on behalf of ATMTA, Inc.
proposals.proposal_text:
# PIP-33: ATMTA Historic Expense Reimbursement

**Category:** Funding
**Author:** Michael Wagner, on behalf of ATMTA, Inc.
**Type:** DAO Treasury
**Date:** June 2026

---

## 2. Summary

This proposal seeks reimbursement of $469,513.53 USD from the Star Atlas DAO Treasury for verified historic expenditures paid by ATMTA on behalf of the Star Atlas DAO covering 2021 tax obligations and payments to two professional services providers.

Reimbursement structured as 75% USDC ($352,135.15) and 25% ATLAS (~$117,378.38 equivalent), split into two equal semi-annual tranches of $234,756.76 each.

Given this exceeds the standard Ecosystem Fund limit, the Star Atlas Foundation is coordinating to bring this as an extraordinary funding measure directly to community vote. Outcome remains entirely in the hands of POLIS holders.

---

## 3. Motivation

During the early phases of the DAO's establishment, before governance infrastructure and treasury processes were fully operational, ATMTA absorbed several significant costs on the DAO's behalf:

|Category|Amount|
|-|-|
|I. 2021 Taxes|$318,092|
|II. Walkers|$142,780|
|III. Leeward|$8,641|
|**Total**|**$469,514**|

These were obligation-level payments required for the DAO's continued lawful and operational existence, not discretionary development costs. ATMTA made them in good faith without the expectation the DAO would eventually reimburse them. However, given continued market under-performance, this capital is now material in business continuity, particularly in light of a significantly reduced cost structure of our team. We believe this reimbursement extends runway by 6-12 months, depending on other internal expense decisions.

Aligns with the DAO's founding principle of **Sustainable Governance**: the DAO should fairly compensate those who supported it.

---

## 4. Specification

Two equal semi-annual tranches:

**Tranche 1 — $234,756.76**

* USDC: $176,067.57
* ATLAS: equivalent to $58,689.19 USD at 7-day VWAP on day prior to execution

**Tranche 2 — $234,756.76** *(180 days after Tranche 1)*

* Same structure

ATLAS pricing: 7-day VWAP of ATLAS/USDC from a designated oracle (e.g., Birdeye / Jupiter TWAP) on the business day preceding each payment, which protects both DAO and ATMTA from short-term manipulation.

* Special Note on Tranche 2: We will assess the value of assets in the DAO Treasury at the time the second payment is due, and only deduct a maximum value up to an amount that retains sufficient capital to cover an additional year of operating costs for the Foundation/DAO.

---

## 5. Stakeholder Impact

|Stakeholder|Benefit|Risk|
|-|-|-|
|POLIS Holders|Settles legitimate obligation, strengthens DAO integrity|Treasury expenditure of $469,513|
|ATMTA|Recoups capital advanced on DAO's behalf|—|
|Foundation|Demonstrates capacity to facilitate extraordinary measures|May face scrutiny given ATMTA's role as sponsor|
|Community|Transparency into early cost management|Perception risk surrounding Material Conflict of Interest in ATMTA writing the proposal and being the sole beneficiary, as well as negative impact to future proposals requesting funding from the Ecosystem Fund|

---

## 6. Implementation Details

Source: DAO Treasury (`CuwnarNh7FEqZMmffFjRyWj54RecyS7zwFg1CxfzNudi`)

**Proposal Draft Period:**

1. **T+0 days:** ATMTA publishes expense documentation (tax filings, invoices, ledger summary) to governance forum at the time proposal draft is submitted (complete)

**Upon Proposal Passing:**

* **T+7 days:** ATMTA confirms and publishes recipient treasury address
* **T+14 days (Tranche 1):** Foundation calculates ATLAS VWAP → executes USDC + ATLAS transfers → publishes on-chain confirmation
* **T+194 days (Tranche 2):** Same process repeated
* **T+194 days:** Foundation posts final transaction link confirmation closing the proposal

No on-chain program changes required — standard SPL token transfers from DAO Treasury.

---

## 7. Timeline

|Milestone|Action|
|-|-|
|Proposal Draft Period||
|T+0|Proposal approved (complete)|
|T+0|Documentation published (complete)|
|Upon Proposal Passing||
|T+7|Recipient address confirmed|
|T+14|**Tranche 1** released ($176,067.57 USDC + $58,689.19 ATLAS equiv.)|
|T+14|Tranche 1 on-chain confirmation posted|
|T+194|**Tranche 2** released|
|T+194|Final transaction link confirmation|

---
